tool_args.label: New York City Transit Procurements - October 2025
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit Procurements. October 26, 2025. Louis Montanti, MTA Deputy Chief Procurement Officer – Procurement Operations. The Procurement Agenda this month includes 2 actions for a proposed estimated expenditure of $393.3M.

payload.title: New York City Transit Procurements
payload.description: Board procurement agenda for NYC Transit including 2 actions for $393.3M: a sole-source parts contract with NFI Parts ($7.1M) and a modification to purchase 45 diesel-battery hybrid locomotives from MotivePower Inc. ($386.2M)

tool_args.label: NFI Parts Replacement Parts Pricing Agreement
tool_args.raw_text: NYC Transit, on behalf of itself and MTA Bus Company (\"MTABC\") (together, the \"Agencies\") requests Board approval to award a sole-source, one-year estimated quantity contract for replacement parts to The Aftermarket Parts Company, LLC d/b/a NFI Parts (\"NFI\") in the estimated value of $7,105,837.

payload.project_name: NFI Parts Replacement Parts Pricing Agreement
payload.description: Award of a sole-source one-year estimated quantity contract for replacement parts from NFI Parts for NYC Transit and MTA Bus Company bus fleets (New Flyer, Orion, MCI)

tool_args.label: Diesel-Battery Hybrid Locomotive Purchase Option (Contract R34255)
tool_args.raw_text: NYC Transit is requesting Board approval to exercise the Option of the contract with MotivePower, Inc., a Wabtec company (\"MPI\") for the purchase of 45 additional diesel-battery hybrid locomotives

payload.description: One-year contract term for NFI Parts replacement parts pricing agreement

tool_args.label: MPI Option Locomotives delivery begins
tool_args.raw_text: Delivery of the Option Locomotives is anticipated to begin May 27, 2027, and be completed by September 20, 2029.

payload.description: Anticipated start of delivery for 45 diesel-battery hybrid option locomotives

tool_args.label: MPI Option Locomotives delivery complete
tool_args.raw_text: Delivery of the Option Locomotives is anticipated to begin May 27, 2027, and be completed by September 20, 2029.

payload.description: Anticipated completion of delivery for 45 diesel-battery hybrid option locomotives

tool_args.label: MPI base contract awarded
tool_args.raw_text: In July 2020, the Board authorized the award of Contract R34255 to MPI for the purchase of 25 diesel-battery hybrid locomotives, including related non-car items at a total price of $233 million.

payload.description: Board authorized award of Contract R34255 to MPI for 25 diesel-battery hybrid locomotives at $233 million

tool_args.label: MPI contract term
tool_args.raw_text: Contract Term (including Options, if any) August 2020–August 2030

payload.description: Contract term for MotivePower Inc. locomotive contract including options

tool_args.label: NFI is sole responsible source
tool_args.raw_text: NFI is the sole responsible source for the parts that will be provided under this Contract. No other vendor can access the proprietary designs, specifications, and drawings and, therefore, cannot readily provide these parts.

payload.claim_text: NFI is the sole responsible source for the parts that will be provided under this Contract. No other vendor can access the proprietary designs, specifications, and drawings and, therefore, cannot readily provide these parts.
payload.subject: NFI Parts

tool_args.label: Multi-agency joint procurement
tool_args.raw_text: The Contract is the result of a multi-agency joint procurement

payload.claim_text: The Contract is the result of a multi-agency joint procurement and provides for the furnishing of various replacement parts that are required for the Agencies to operate and maintain their respective fleets of buses.
payload.subject: NFI Parts Pricing Agreement

tool_args.label: Option Locomotives purpose
tool_args.raw_text: The Option Locomotives, like the locomotives in the base contract, are required to conduct critical in-house track maintenance and work in support of the Capital Plan

payload.claim_text: The Option Locomotives, like the locomotives in the base contract, are required to conduct critical in-house track maintenance and work in support of the Capital Plan as they transport flatcars bearing materials to, and debris from, worksites and provide support for work performed within the transit system by outside contractors.
payload.subject: Diesel-Battery Hybrid Locomotives

tool_args.label: Option Locomotives replace aging fleet
tool_args.raw_text: These R34255 Option Locomotives will replace R37, R41, R43, R52 and R77 locomotives (all built between 1966 and 1983 and are beyond their useful lives of 35 years)

payload.claim_text: These R34255 Option Locomotives will replace R37, R41, R43, R52 and R77 locomotives (all built between 1966 and 1983 and are beyond their useful lives of 35 years) in the NYC Transit fleet.
payload.subject: Diesel-Battery Hybrid Locomotives

tool_args.label: Warranty extended to 36 months
tool_args.raw_text: an extension to the original 24-month warranty for the base locomotives to 36-months

payload.claim_text: NYC Transit was able to negotiate a reduction in the overall Option price with MPI based on a reduction of bonding requirements as well as an extension to the original 24-month warranty for the base locomotives to 36-months.
payload.subject: MotivePower Inc. Option Contract
